steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is a notes-based writing question. The task is to make a generalization about the materials used in dhow replicas. The decisive clues in the notes are: contemporary builders “use a mix of modern and traditional materials” and “most of the materials used are traditional.” The response must capture that broad balance — not a single example, not facts about ancient dhows, not just who builds replicas.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — A traditional material that was used to stitch together the hulls of ancient dhows, coconut palm fiber rope is still used by shipbuilders.: highlights one specific traditional material (coconut palm fiber rope) rather than making a general statement about the overall material composition of replicas. ✗
  • Choice B — The ancient Arab dhow was a sailing vessel used primarily for trade and distinguishable by its triangular sails.: describes the ancient vessel’s features; says nothing about materials used in modern replicas. ✗
  • Choice C — Although most materials used in dhow replicas are traditional, some modern materials are used.: directly generalizes the material composition — most are traditional, some are modern — matching the notes exactly. ✓
  • Choice D — Contemporary shipbuilders in Oman build replicas of the dhow, which was an ancient sailing vessel with a stitched hull construction.: introduces who builds replicas and what a dhow is, but makes no generalization about the materials used. ✗
C is correct.
